blocks.content: Not a fade — just cut. One beat it was there, the next it wasn't, and the silence landed like something physical.
They were still close. Still right there, a breath apart, both of them in that half-second between what almost happened and what was going to happen next.
Kit looked at her. Daisy looked at him.
Not the teasing smile, not the polite one. The small, private one. His mouth curved to match it.
For just that moment it was only the two of them, smiling like idiots.

blocks.speaker: DJ
blocks.content: Alright Millhaven — hope y'all caught your breath out there, 'cause we are NOT slowing down again tonight!
The lights kicked up. A wave of noise rolled across the floor. Someone near the punch table cheered. The gym came back all at once.
Eli appeared at Kit's shoulder, materializing from the crowd the way he always did.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I leave for ten minutes and the DJ gets on the mic to personally ruin everyone's evening. What did I miss.
Kit looked at Daisy. She was still smiling, looking down now, her hand back at her side.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Tragic. Come on, I need water and someone to talk to.

He put his hand on Kit's back and steered him toward the edge of the floor. Kit went. He looked back once — Daisy already falling into step behind them, catching his eye when he turned, the smile still there at the corners of her mouth.
The next song started. Fast, loud, everything back to normal.
